article_title: Passkey Adoption Hits Inflection Point as UAE Bans SMS OTP and Microsoft Auto-Enables Entra Profiles

content_preview.title: Passkey Adoption Hits Inflection Point as UAE Bans SMS OTP and Microsoft Auto-Enables Entra Profiles
content_preview.summary: The UAE mandates all banks abandon SMS OTP by March 31 while Microsoft auto-enables passkey profiles across Entra ID, as FIDO Alliance data shows 87 percent of enterprises now deploying passkeys.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Two converging developments in March 2026 signal that passkey adoption has crossed from early-adopter enthusiasm into institutional mandate. The Central Bank of the UAE has imposed a [March 31 deadline](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-entra-brings-phishing-resistant-sign-in-to-windows/) for all licensed financial institutions to eliminate SMS and email one-time passwords, making it the first country to outright ban OTP-based authentication in its banking se...

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive News article at 988 words covering passkey adoption from regulatory (UAE), enterprise (Microsoft), and industry (FIDO Alliance) angles. Well-structured with clear sections and appropriate technical depth. The article effectively connects institutional mandates with adoption statistics.
editor_notes.source_verification: Source 1 (bleepingcomputer.com): Bot-blocked (HTTP 403) — cannot verify. URL slug suggests Microsoft Entra focus, but the article attributes UAE OTP ban claims to it. Cannot confirm whether this source covers UAE banking regulations. Source 2 (devblogs.microsoft.com): Fetched successfully — this April 2025 engineering connect post covers External ID features (custom OIDC providers, app creation restrictions), NOT passkey auto-enabling or synced passkey profiles. The article's claim about Microsoft auto-enabling passkey profiles is misattributed to this source. Source 3 (fidoalliance.org): Page loaded but content was JavaScript-rendered and not extractable — cannot verify the specific statistics (15 billion accounts, 87% enterprise deployment, 93% success rate, etc.). Source 4 (nextgov.com): Confirmed behavioral biometrics discussion, C2PA open standards, typing rhythm/device handling monitoring, and digital identity approaches.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The core claims about passkey adoption, UAE OTP ban, and Microsoft Entra passkey features are consistent with known industry developments and widely reported. However, Source 2 (devblogs) does not support the claims attributed to it — it covers different Entra ID features. The specific FIDO Alliance statistics could not be independently verified due to JavaScript-heavy rendering. The factual content is likely accurate but source attribution is imprecise.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No promotional language despite covering specific vendor implementations.
editor_notes.originality: Novel coverage of converging passkey adoption milestones. No prior coverage found in the archive.
concerns: Source 2 (devblogs.microsoft.com/identity/eng-connect-apr-2025) does not contain information about passkey auto-enabling or synced passkeys — it covers External ID features. This is a source misattribution.
Source 1 (BleepingComputer) is bot-blocked and its URL suggests Microsoft focus, but UAE banking claims are attributed to it — unverifiable.
recommendations: Replace Source 2 with the actual Microsoft announcement about Entra ID passkey profiles auto-enabling.
Verify that Source 1 indeed covers the UAE Central Bank OTP ban, or replace with a direct UAE regulatory source.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-written article with plausible factual claims, but source attribution has issues. Source 2 definitively does not support the claims attributed to it. Despite this, the underlying news events (UAE OTP ban, Microsoft passkey rollout, FIDO statistics) are well-established industry developments. Approved with noted concerns about source-claim mapping — the errors appear to be citation mistakes rather than fabrication.
